rules.title: Text not included in an ARIA landmark
rules.description: All perceivable text content should be included in an ARIA landmark. Perceivable text content refers to text that can be perceived by users of assistive technology — some of which may be invisible to sighted users.

rules.title: Interactive element does not meet enhanced size
rules.description: Interactive elements, like buttons or links, should be at least 44 by 44 pixels, so they can easily be clicked or tapped by all users.

rules.title: Color contrast does not meet enhanced requirement
rules.description: There is insufficient color contrast between the text and background on this page to meet the enhanced contrast requirement.

rules.title: Interactive element does not meet minimum size nor spacing
rules.description:
Interactive elements, like buttons or links, should be large enough or be positioned, so they can easily be clicked or tapped by users.

Elements should be at least 24 by 24 pixels, or if smaller, be positioned with enough spacing to not intersect with other interactive elements.

rules.title: Line height is below minimum value
rules.description: The `line-height` for paragraphs should be at least 1.5 times the font size.

rules.title: Color contrast does not meet minimum requirement
rules.description: There is insufficient color contrast between the text and background on this page to meet the minimum contrast requirement.

rules.title: Font size is fixed
rules.description: The `font-size` has been fixed using an absolute length unit, such as `px`, `pt` or `cm`.

rules.title: Link missing a text alternative
rules.description: Links should always have a text alternative. The link text can be inserted into the HTML — it does **not** need to be visible to pass this check.

rules.title: Line height is fixed
rules.description: The `line-height` has been fixed using an absolute length unit, such as `px`, `pt` or `cm`.

rules.title: Headings are not structured
rules.description: One or more heading levels have been skipped.

rules.title: Links are not clearly identifiable
rules.description: Color alone is not enough to distinguish a link from the surrounding text.

rules.title: Image missing a text alternative
rules.description: All non-decorative images must have a text alternative (also known as "alt text").

rules.title: ARIA attribute unsupported or prohibited
rules.description: An ARIA attribute has been specified that is either not supported or is prohibited, on this type of element.

rules.title: Overuse of italics
rules.description: The `font-style` property has been used to display an entire paragraph in italics.

rules.title: Container element is empty
rules.description: Some roles are designed to contain other roles. This element has this type of role, but it doesn't contain any required-owned elements.

rules.title: Role with implied hidden content has keyboard focus
rules.description:
Some elements have a role that implies their internal structure should be hidden from assistive technologies. In other words, their sub-elements have a presentational role.

If a sub-element has keyboard focus, it should be moved outside of the `presentation` role.

rules.title: Form field missing a label
rules.description: Every form element should have a descriptive text label.

rules.title: Content missing after heading
rules.description:
Headings are used to structure content in a hierarchy. This helps users to know where to start and how content sections relate to one another.

Content should appear as expected including between headings of the same level and after the last section or page heading.

rules.title: Text in all caps
rules.description: The `text-transform` property has been used to display an entire paragraph in uppercase (capital letters).

rules.title: Empty headings
rules.description: This page contains one or more "empty" headings (heading tags with no text content).

rules.title: Page does not start with a level 1 heading
rules.description:
A level 1 heading `h1` tells the user what the page is about before they decide to navigate through the content. 

Headings are used to structure content into a hierarchy of importance. If a page starts with a different heading level, it can cause confusion for the user.

rules.title: Presentational element is exposed to assistive technologies
rules.description:
An element has been marked as decorative, but it remains exposed to assistive technologies.

This may be because:
- the element has an ARIA attribute (these contain "instructions" for assistive technologies), or
- the element is focusable (meaning it can still be reached by keyboard)

rules.title: Decorative image is exposed to assistive technologies
rules.description:
This image or SVG has been marked as decorative, but it remains exposed to assistive technologies.

This could be because:
- the image has a text alternative or other ARIA attribute (ARIA attributes contain "instructions" for assistive technologies), or
- the image is focusable (meaning it can still be reached by keyboard)

rules.title: Visible label and accessible name do not match
rules.description: The accessible name of any interactive element should contain its visible text label.

rules.title: Role not inside the required context
rules.description: This page element has a `role` that only makes sense in the context of another `role`.

rules.title: Uneven spacing in text
rules.description: Text should not be "justified" (aligned to both margins). Instead, it should be aligned to the left, right or center.

rules.title: Inline frame missing a text alternative
rules.description: Every inline frame (`iframe`) should have a text alternative that summarizes its content or purpose.

rules.title: Scrollable element is not keyboard accessible
rules.description: This page has a scrollable section that can't be operated by keyboard.

rules.title: Table cell missing context
rules.description:
Data tables include header and data cells arranged in a grid.

Each data cell `<td>` should be assigned to at least one column or row header cell `<th>`. When this is done, a screen reader can announce each data cell in context.

rules.title: Improper use of preformatted text element
rules.description:
The preformatted text element `<pre>` tells the browser to leave text formatting as it is, preventing the user from adjusting letter spacing. It should only be used for content that is either code or a `<figure>` such as ASCII art. 

Without exception, the content of this element is meant to be visually presented to a sighted user.

rules.title: Links on the same page with the same text alternative
rules.description: Two or more links on this page have the exact same link text — despite pointing to different URLs.

rules.title: Page missing headings
rules.description: This page does not appear to have any heading tags (`h1` through to `h6`), or any other elements with the `role` of heading.

rules.title: HTML element is deprecated or obsolete
rules.description: HTML elements are updated over time and older versions are deprecated before becoming obsolete. If a deprecated or obsolete element is used, it may create usability issues. - Learn more about deprecated elements

rules.title: Button missing a text alternative
rules.description: Buttons should always have a text label. The label can be inserted into the HTML — it does **not** need to be visible to pass this check.

rules.title: Skip to main content link is missing
rules.description: The first item reached by keyboard should be a link to the main content of the page. This link should be visible when receiving focus from the keyboard.

rules.title: Grouped form controls missing an accessible name
rules.description:
Grouped form controls can include elements such as checkboxes or radio buttons where the user needs to make a selection.

Each group should have an accessible name so that screen reader users can understand the group’s purpose.

rules.title: Hidden element has focusable content
rules.description: A page element with the attribute `aria-hidden="true"` contains focusable elements (elements that can receive focus from a keyboard).

rules.title: Page language has not been identified
rules.description: The main language of the page has not been declared within the `<html>` element.

rules.title: Vector image missing a text alternative
rules.description: All non-decorative images should have a descriptive text alternative.

rules.title: Page missing a title
rules.description:
Every page needs a descriptive title. 

You should be able to add a page title from your CMS without needing to access the source code.

rules.title: Font size is too small
rules.description: The `font-size` should not be smaller than 9 px, or `xx-small`.

rules.title: Table header cell is missing a header role
rules.description: A table header cell should have the role of `columnheader` or `rowheader` as this describes its relationship to other cells in the table.

rules.title: Links in the same context with the same text alternative
rules.description: Two or more links in the same context have the exact same link text — despite pointing to different pages.

rules.title: No data cells assigned to table header
rules.description: Every header cell in a table should have at least one data cell assigned to it.

rules.title: Page region missing an accessible name
rules.description: Any page section with the role of `region` should also have an accessible name to identify it.

rules.title: ARIA attribute does not exist
rules.description: The specified `aria-*` attribute does not exist in WAI-ARIA. It may have been mistyped.

rules.title: Required ARIA attribute is missing
rules.description: This element has a `role` that should be accompanied by specific ARIA attributes.

rules.title: Summary element missing an accessible name
rules.description: `summary` elements must include clear and descriptive text that explains the content of the collapsible `details` section.

rules.title: Invalid state or property
rules.description: An ARIA state or property has an invalid value. It may have been mistyped.

rules.title: Table headers aren't referenced correctly
rules.description: The `headers` attribute for each data cell should reference a header cell in the same table. It may have been mistyped.

rules.title: Page zoom is restricted
rules.description: The `viewport` element on this page prevents users from zooming in or scaling text content.

rules.title: Object missing a text alternative
rules.description: An embedded `<object>` is missing a text alternative.

rules.title: Image button missing a text alternative
rules.description: Every image button needs a text alternative ("alt text") describing its purpose on the page.

rules.title: All roles are invalid
rules.description: None of the roles assigned to this element are defined in WAI-ARIA.

rules.title: Some roles are invalid
rules.description: Only some of the roles assigned to this element are defined in WAI-ARIA.
